title: Specify what makes an integration seamless
summary: AI-generated copy describes every integration as 'seamless' without specifying what eliminates the seam: one-click setup, automatic mapping, or no configuration.
instruction: When describing an integration, specify the seam it eliminates: 'One-click import with automatic field mapping' rather than 'Seamless integration'.
rationale: 'Seamless' is a reflexive modifier that communicates no specific benefit. Users need to know what makes the integration easy: no config, auto-mapping, live sync, or single sign-on. The term itself passes the evaluation to the reader.

violations: 'Seamless integration with your existing tools' without specifics about how.
'Seamless CRM sync' without describing sync frequency or conflict resolution.
controls: 'Seamless Stripe integration: connect your account in one click with automatic tax calculation, receipt generation, and subscription management.'
'Pre-built Jira integration with automatic field mapping and bidirectional sync.'
borderline: 'Seamless' in clothing ('seamless socks') has a literal physical meaning — product copy using the same word in a different context may be confusing.

exceptions.description: Landing pages where multiple integrations are listed in a grid and each opens to a detailed page with specifics — the grid itself may use 'seamless' as a category header.

detector.signalDescription: Match 'seamless' in feature/integration descriptions. Check for specific integration details within the same section (one-click, auto, setup, sync, import).
limitations: Does not apply to literal 'seamless' usage in non-software contexts.
Integration detail may be on a linked page rather than inline.

scenarios: A textile/clothing context where 'seamless' is literal — requires context detection.
An integration marketplace where each card says 'Seamless integration' but clicking opens detailed setup docs — evidence lives in adjacent doc, not inline.
A product page linking to a dedicated setup guide where the integration specifics are documented — the landing page uses 'seamless' as a summary header.
mitigations: Check for actionable integration detail (click-to-connect buttons, setup docs, integration guides) on the page or linked from it. Flag only when 'seamless' is the only descriptor with no mechanism evidence in the section or adjacent documentation.

copyPattern.replacementStrategy: Describe the specific integration mechanism instead of 'seamless': one-click setup, automatic field mapping, no-code connection, bidirectional sync, or SSO authentication.

fixturePlan.description: A page listing 'Seamless integration with Salesforce, HubSpot, and Slack' with no setup details.
Same page with 'Connect Salesforce in one click with automatic contact and deal mapping.'
An integration marketplace where grid items say 'Seamless integration' but each links to detailed setup docs.
